entries.title: Immediate postwar is not epilogue state

context.label: Immediate postwar before settled adult epilogue

content.fact: Immediately after the Battle of Hogwarts, characters are in grief, recovery, reconstruction, and political transition; they are not yet in the settled 2017 epilogue family state.
content.injection: In immediate postwar Context, preserve trauma, funerals, repairs, and institutional cleanup; do not jump to married-with-children epilogue assumptions unless the active Context is the epilogue.
constraints: Use this as a story-position gate, not a general encyclopedia fact.
If the active Context is before this window, block this state unless accepted story lore explicitly changed it.
Apply only when Saga Context matches Immediate postwar before settled adult epilogue; if the scene is earlier, withhold this fact or state.
antiLore: Do not import later-book knowledge, deaths, relationships, or reveals into this Context.
Do not let generic model memory override the active Saga Context.
Do not let generic model memory, film shorthand, fanon, or later-book knowledge override the active book Context.
Do not reveal, assume, or discuss this outcome before the relevant anchor/window is active unless the user has created an AU branch.

sourceInfo.title: Post-War and Epilogue
sourceInfo.notes: Relevance-curated Saga development pass; verify in Deck Health and human review.

entries.title: Next generation appears only in epilogue Context

content.fact: Before the 2017 King’s Cross epilogue Context, the next-generation children should not be active Hogwarts students or used to define immediate postwar scenes.
content.injection: Block Albus Severus, Rose, Hugo, James Sirius, Lily Luna, and Scorpius school-era assumptions until the epilogue window is active or an AU timeskip is declared. Block contradictory later-book or earlier-state knowledge unless accepted story lore explicitly overrides it.
constraints: Use this as a story-position gate, not a general encyclopedia fact.
